P3MKT-001.title: 2025 AI Marketing Industry Report
P3MKT-001.author: Social Media Examiner (n=730 marketers)
P3MKT-001.excerpt: 60% of marketers now use AI tools daily, up from 37% in 2024. 84% increased their AI adoption over the past year. ChatGPT leads at 90% adoption, Google Gemini follows at 51%, Claude reaches 33% usage. Marketers leverage AI for brainstorming (90%), drafting (89%), and headline composition (86%). 36% of marketers worry about AI replacing their roles. 79% want automation workflow expertise, 69% seek video content creation skills.
P3MKT-001.relevance: Anchor stat for AI penetration depth and tool concentration in marketing
P3MKT-001.credibility: high - Social Media Examiner has run this annual survey for years, decent sample

P3MKT-002.title: State of AI in Marketing Report 2025
P3MKT-002.author: CoSchedule (n=1005)
P3MKT-002.excerpt: 85% of marketers use AI tools for content creation. 83.82% experienced increased productivity. 74.6% feel AI provides competitive advantage. 25.6% report AI-generated content outperforms manually created; 64% indicate AI content performs equally or better. Top barrier: data privacy (40.44%). 85.84% plan to increase AI usage in 2-3 years. 84% report no declines in team size despite AI adoption.
P3MKT-002.relevance: Large sample for adoption baseline; 'team size flat' contrasts with layoff narrative

P3MKT-003.title: 65% of Marketing Jobs May Not Survive AI
P3MKT-003.author: Mark Ritson (Adweek)
P3MKT-003.excerpt: 65% of marketing tasks are replaceable with AI operations (Anthropic report). Marketing ranked 5th most vulnerable among 800 occupations. US marketing job postings fell 7% YoY and 15% QoQ in Q2 2025. Hiring of younger workers in AI-exposed roles dropped 14% versus 2022. Marketing salaries flat in 2025, declining in real terms. Most vulnerable: market research, consumer research interpretation, data analysis, customer journey mapping, report writing.
P3MKT-003.relevance: Counter-evidence to rosy adoption narrative; Ritson is credible CMO/academic voice
P3MKT-003.credibility: high - Mark Ritson is well-known marketing academic, Adweek is industry-standard

P3MKT-004.title: AI's impact on early-career marketers is reaching a crisis point
P3MKT-004.author: MarTech (citing Paul Roetzer / Marketing AI Institute)
P3MKT-004.excerpt: Paul Roetzer: 'If you were just executing (building landing pages, writing copy, etc.), you're cooked. That is not a job one to two years out.' BCG research: 90% of marketing manager tasks disrupted from a skills perspective. 16% relative employment decline among workers ages 22-25 post-ChatGPT. Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei estimates 50% of entry-level white-collar jobs could be eliminated within five years.
P3MKT-004.relevance: Strongest signal that execution-tier marketing roles being hollowed out fast; junior pipeline broken
P3MKT-004.credibility: high - direct quotes from Roetzer (Marketing AI Institute founder)

P3MKT-005.title: Trouble Brews: Fewer Entry-Level Marketing Hires
P3MKT-005.author: Content Marketing Institute
P3MKT-005.excerpt: One in three marketers report their organizations are reducing entry-level hiring. Net hiring score: -19.8 points, with reductions outpacing increases 2.5x. 75% of employed marketers say finding marketing jobs is significantly or somewhat more challenging than two years ago. Avg job search: 5.2 months (up from 3.1 in 2024). Advice for entry-level: 'Build visible work demonstrating judgment rather than credentials alone.'
P3MKT-005.relevance: Junior marketer pipeline crisis = product opportunity for 'show your AI work' platform
P3MKT-005.credibility: high - CMI is the leading content marketing publication

P3MKT-006.title: AI threatens entry-level marketing jobs—and the future talent pipeline
P3MKT-006.author: MarTech
P3MKT-006.excerpt: Technology companies cut employees aged 21-25 by 50% over two years. Average employee age at major tech companies increased from 34.3 to 39.4. Recent college graduate unemployment (22-27): 5.8%. Organizations now prioritize candidates with 'AI experience beyond basic prompt writing.' Agencies are refocusing junior positions toward campaign planning, guiding teams on AI adoption, and innovating with new AI capabilities. 75.8% of 3,500+ marketers identify AI expertise as a major skills gap, yet only 5% are actively building generative AI capabilities.
P3MKT-006.relevance: Confirms employers want signal beyond prompt writing; explicit signal gap = product opportunity

P3MKT-007.title: The rise of vibe marketing and what it means for marketers
P3MKT-007.author: MarTech
P3MKT-007.excerpt: Vibe marketing uses AI and no-code tools to transform plain-language ideas into live campaigns. The vibe marketer represents 'a single marketer can be part-engineer, part-designer, and part-analyst.' Tools: Bolt, Lovable, Make.com, ChatGPT, Claude, Midjourney, AI agents. Phoenix and YC startups hiring 'Vibe Marketers' with comp up to $1M. Interest in vibe marketing surged nearly 7x in 2025. MarTech Conference 2026 launching dedicated Vibe Marketing Lab.
P3MKT-007.relevance: Emerging identity for AI-native marketer; concrete tool stack and salary signal
P3MKT-007.credibility: medium-high - well-known industry pub but trend-y framing

P3MKT-008.title: Vibe Marketer becomes #1 new marketing role
P3MKT-008.author: Jon (growth substack)
P3MKT-008.excerpt: Ramp posted a 'Vibe Marketing Manager' role. Vibe marketers possess: technical versatility (build landing pages, video ads, analysis independently), full-stack capabilities, AI fluency (GPTs, agents, LLMs), autonomy (ship MVPs without engineering), generalist mentality. Tools showcased: Retool, Bubble, Zapier, Vercel, Gumloop, Lovable. Example: 'former growth lead who now builds tools with Gumloop or Lovable.'
P3MKT-008.relevance: Concrete role evidence (Ramp posting) + skill requirement list
P3MKT-008.credibility: medium - individual substack, but cites real job postings

P3MKT-009.title: Meet the marketer of the future: The Gen Marketer
P3MKT-009.author: Emily Kramer (MKT1, ex-Asana marketing lead)
P3MKT-009.excerpt: Gen Marketer = marketing generalist for the generative AI era. 5 characteristics: (1) AI Orchestration Expert managing hybrid people+agent teams, (2) Audience-Centric Strategy, (3) Campaign Production across channels, (4) Π-shaped skillset (depth in 2+ subfunctions), (5) Adaptive Intelligence. 'AI won't create more specialists. It will create more generalists.' Predicts traditional specialist roles will shrink or disappear.
P3MKT-009.relevance: Authoritative framework from Emily Kramer (ex-Asana, MKT1 founder); cited across industry
P3MKT-009.credibility: high - Kramer is the most-cited voice on B2B marketing org design

P3MKT-010.title: How AI could make you more valuable
P3MKT-010.author: Kyle Poyar (Growth Unhinged, 84K subs)
P3MKT-010.excerpt: 'AI hasn't necessarily spelled the death of human-made content. What it's done is make the top 1% writer much more valuable.' AI bifurcates the market - commodifies mediocre work while elevating top-tier talent. OpenAI paying $400K for content strategist role. Top marketers shift from content creation to 'strategic content engineering' - 'from being mentioned by ChatGPT to convincing ChatGPT to say the right things about your product.' Super embeds preset LLM prompts in website footer.
P3MKT-010.relevance: Bifurcation thesis - critical nuance for product strategy. Top tier visible & desired; long tail commodified
P3MKT-010.credibility: high - Kyle Poyar is well-respected former OpenView partner, top growth voice

P3MKT-011.title: AI Marketing Specialist Skills in 2025
P3MKT-011.author: Teal
P3MKT-011.excerpt: Top AI marketing skills: AI tool proficiency (Jasper, HubSpot AI, Salesforce Einstein), data analytics, prompt engineering, marketing automation, ML fundamentals. Hybrid roles 'blur the lines between strategist, technologist, and creative.' Marketing professionals with AI skills see salary increases of 20-30% over traditional counterparts. Senior AI specialists earning up to $250K total comp. In marketing/sales specifically, applied AI skills trigger ~43% pay bumps.
P3MKT-011.credibility: medium - aggregator/career site, but skill taxonomy reasonable

P3MKT-012.title: Upwork's In-Demand Skills 2026: Demand for AI Skills More Than Doubles
P3MKT-012.author: Upwork Inc
P3MKT-012.excerpt: Skills explicitly tied to applying AI within existing roles grew 109% YoY. Specific growth: AI video gen/edit +329%, AI integration +178%, AI image gen/edit +95%, data annotation +154%, AI chatbot dev +71%. Digital marketing, translation, localization show significant increases as companies seek human expertise to enhance AI efforts. 58% of businesses will prioritize AI proficiency when hiring freelancers.
P3MKT-012.relevance: Freelance marketing channel is one of the most direct 'AI capability is hireable signal' markets
P3MKT-012.credibility: high - first-party data from Upwork, the largest freelance platform

P3MKT-013.title: Jasper in Review: How Marketers Used Jasper in 2025
P3MKT-013.author: Jasper AI
P3MKT-013.excerpt: 76M+ generations on Jasper in 2025. Millions of campaigns launched. 100+ specialized AI agents. Jasper positioned as 'core engine for content creation, powering campaign launches to AEO/GEO/SEO content refreshes.' Late 2025 launching hyper-personalization features creating unique content variations for individual users.
P3MKT-013.relevance: Concrete usage scale at a marketing-vertical AI tool
P3MKT-013.credibility: medium - vendor self-report, but order-of-magnitude useful

P3MKT-014.title: 2025 State of Marketing AI Report
P3MKT-014.author: Marketing AI Institute (n=~1900)
P3MKT-014.excerpt: Survey of nearly 1,900 marketers. Report covers AI understanding, usage, buying behavior, top desired outcomes, key adoption barriers, sentiment, AI ownership structures within organizations. Actual statistics behind paywall but referenced widely.
P3MKT-014.relevance: Most authoritative dedicated marketing-AI survey; cited widely
P3MKT-014.credibility: high - Marketing AI Institute (Paul Roetzer) is the dedicated authority

P3MKT-015.title: Reinventing marketing workflows with agentic AI
P3MKT-015.author: McKinsey & Company
P3MKT-015.excerpt: Agentic AI systems built on foundation models capable of acting and executing multistep processes enable organizations to create hybrid human-agentic workforces in which one marketing professional can supervise a team of agents, potentially driving growth and boosting productivity. Companies utilizing AI for marketing experiencing a 37% reduction in costs and a 39% increase in revenue when moving beyond point solutions.
P3MKT-015.relevance: Top-tier consultant signal that marketing workflows being structurally redesigned, not just augmented

P3MKT-016.title: Top 20 Marketing Layoffs Due to AI Statistics 2026
P3MKT-016.author: Amra & Elma
P3MKT-016.excerpt: Over 100,000 employees impacted by AI-driven layoffs in 2025. 1/3 of CMOs expect to cut staff over next 1-2 years. ~50% of marketing leaders at largest companies anticipate layoffs. 69% of firms adopting GenAI marketing tools (2024-2025) fully terminated or substantially reduced external marketing agency, BPO, or offshore content studio contracts within 18 months.
P3MKT-016.relevance: Quantifies agency-side displacement (critical for freelance market dynamics)
P3MKT-016.credibility: medium - aggregator; numbers consistent with other sources but unverified primary source

P3MKT-017.title: How to Build a Marketing Portfolio That Gets Clients in 2026
P3MKT-017.author: Lovable
P3MKT-017.excerpt: Modern marketing portfolio essentials: 3-5 detailed case studies organized by problem-solution-results framework, not by output category. Showcase paid work, pro bono, spec work, personal brand campaigns. 'Portfolios with quantified achievements generate 2.5 times more interview callbacks.' Personal brand campaigns (LinkedIn series, newsletters) serve as 'live case studies.' Does not extensively address AI capability showcasing.
P3MKT-017.relevance: Confirms portfolio culture exists but is organized around results/campaigns, NOT around AI workflows - gap = opportunity
P3MKT-017.credibility: medium - vendor (Lovable) but generally aligned with portfolio orthodoxy

P3MKT-018.title: Advertising, Promotions, and Marketing Managers - Occupational Outlook
P3MKT-018.author: US Bureau of Labor Statistics
P3MKT-018.excerpt: Advertising and promotions managers held about 27,000 jobs in 2024. Marketing managers held about 407,000 jobs in 2024. Median annual wage marketing managers: $161,030 May 2024. Projected to grow 6% 2024-2034. ~36,400 openings per year projected.
P3MKT-018.relevance: Market size grounding (managers only; total marketers larger)

P3MKT-019.title: Marketing Industry Stats and Information
P3MKT-019.author: American Marketing Association
P3MKT-019.excerpt: AMA estimates 2.5 million marketers in the United States, with 6 million when counting marketing-adjacent roles. AMA takes average of BLS and LinkedIn counts because BLS likely undercounts due to outdated job categories.
P3MKT-019.relevance: TAM grounding - true number of marketing-identified professionals is ~2.5-6M US alone

P3MKT-020.title: How to hire & get hired in 2026: The guide to the Gen Marketer skillset
P3MKT-020.author: Emily Kramer (MKT1)
P3MKT-020.excerpt: Premium piece (paywalled after intro) covering specific Gen Marketer skills, learning steps, interview questions to assess, and curated open roles. Free section: 'This isn't about abandoning specialization' but rather 'layering generalist skills on top of your depth to stay valuable.'
P3MKT-020.relevance: Confirms hiring methodology shifting; specific interview frameworks emerging
P3MKT-020.credibility: high - MKT1 has direct hiring experience at scale-up B2B

P3MKT-021.title: Generative engine optimization (GEO): How to win AI mentions
P3MKT-021.author: Search Engine Land
P3MKT-021.excerpt: GEO is an emerging discipline that shifts content strategy from traditional search rankings to visibility within AI-generated answers and chatbot responses. GEO builds on SEO fundamentals but shifts focus to brand mentions/citations inside AI-generated answers. Companies codifying prompts/QA, creating reusable prompts for briefs, outlines, meta while pairing with human editorial QA.
P3MKT-021.relevance: GEO = new sub-discipline born from AI; concrete example of new marketing capability category
P3MKT-021.credibility: high - Search Engine Land is the authority for search marketing

P3MKT-022.title: 2026 Creator Economy Report
P3MKT-022.author: The Influencer Marketing Factory
P3MKT-022.excerpt: 91.9% of creators now utilize at least one AI tool in their creative workflow. AI acting as a 'productivity multiplier' to automate repetitive tasks while creators focus on authentic storytelling. By mid-2026, TikTok may implement aggressive AI content restrictions due to user demand for authentic human content. Creator middle class emerging.
P3MKT-022.relevance: Creator economy is adjacent marketing segment with near-saturation AI use - but tension around authenticity
P3MKT-022.credibility: medium-high - dedicated creator economy research firm

P3MKT-023.title: AI marketing skills rising 340% on LinkedIn
P3MKT-023.author: LinkedIn (cited)
P3MKT-023.excerpt: LinkedIn reports a 340% increase in job postings requiring AI marketing skills over the past 18 months. Over 70% of marketing roles in 2026 require hands-on experience with AI tools and analytics platforms.
P3MKT-023.relevance: Direct hiring demand signal at scale
P3MKT-023.credibility: medium - LinkedIn data cited via secondary source; direct LinkedIn report would be stronger

P3MKT-024.title: Marketing Agency Case Study: AI Workflows Cut Content Costs by 80%
P3MKT-024.author: Cliprise
P3MKT-024.excerpt: Marketing agency case studies: Adore Me reduced product description generation from 20 hours to 20 minutes per batch, cut localized launch time from months to 10 days, 36% reduction in stylist note writing time. TRY communications group: staff spend 30% less time on repetitive tasks, create proposals 40% faster. Agencies that hit ceiling at 15 clients used AI to scale without major hiring.
P3MKT-024.credibility: medium - vendor case study but specific metrics

P3MKT-025.title: 25 Best AI Marketing Tools for Smarter Workflows
P3MKT-025.author: Sprout Social
P3MKT-025.excerpt: 2025 Sprout Social Index: 97% of marketing leaders say AI proficiency is vital for proper job performance. Companies exploring AI agents that independently execute multi-step marketing workflows.
P3MKT-025.credibility: medium-high - Sprout Social runs respected annual social index
